methods.description: Description of the experimental layout, factors, and replications used for the study.

steps.description: The experiment was conducted in randomised block design with two factors and three replications
steps.searchable_summary: Implement a Randomized Block Design (RBD) for the experiment.

steps.description: The experiment consists of K12 D10 36-1 as male parent and genotype K12 D10 32-5 as female parent for brinjal hybrid seed production. The experiment was conducted in randomised block design with two factors and three replications, The time of pollination as one factor viz., pollination between 6.00 to 7.00 am (T 1 ), 7.00 am to 8.00 am (T 2 ), 8.00 to 9.00 am (T 3 ), 9.00 to 10.00 am (T 4 ) and 10.00 to 11.00 am (T 5 ) and period of pollen storage as another factor viz., Pollination with fresh pollen (P 1 ), pollination in one day old pollen (P 2 ), pollination with two day old pollen (P 3 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Define the two experimental factors: Time of pollination (5 levels) and Period of pollen storage (3 levels).

steps.description: The experiment was conducted in randomised block design with two factors and three replications
steps.searchable_summary: Specify the number of replications as three.

steps.description: The time of pollination as one factor viz., pollination between 6.00 to 7.00 am (T 1 ), 7.00 am to 8.00 am (T 2 ), 8.00 to 9.00 am (T 3 ), 9.00 to 10.00 am (T 4 ) and 10.00 to 11.00 am (T 5 )
steps.searchable_summary: Define the specific time intervals for the Time of pollination factor levels.
outputs: Defined time levels (6-7 am, 7-8 am, 8-9 am, 9-10 am, 10-11 am)

steps.description: period of pollen storage as another factor viz., Pollination with fresh pollen (P 1 ), pollination in one day old pollen (P 2 ), pollination with two day old pollen (P 3 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Define the specific pollen storage durations for the Period of pollen storage factor levels.
outputs: Defined pollen storage levels (fresh, 1 day old, 2 days old)

methods.description: Process for removing the anthers from female parent flowers to prevent self-pollination before controlled pollination.

steps.description: The hermophrodite flowers of female parent were emasculated in the previous evening. Only the floral buds likely to open on next day morning hours were only emasculated
steps.searchable_summary: Identify hermaphrodite floral buds on the female parent likely to open the following morning.

steps.description: The hermophrodite flowers of female parent were emasculated in the previous evening.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform emasculation (anther removal) on the identified floral buds in the evening prior to opening.

steps.description: emasculated by using the forceps, needle, scalpel, etc.
steps.searchable_summary: Utilize tools such as forceps, needle, and scalpel for the emasculation process.

steps.description: Since female parent was indeterminate in growth habit, the emasculation work was restricted only to first 30 days from start of flowering period wherein flowering was at its peak.
steps.searchable_summary: Restrict emasculation activities to the first 30 days from the start of the flowering period.
outputs: Emasculated buds within peak flowering period

methods.description: Process for collecting pollen from male parent flowers and preparing it for immediate use or storage.

steps.description: At the start of blooming period, fully opened flowers were plucked from the male parental block and were collected in polythene bag in early morning hours of the crossing day.
steps.searchable_summary: Pluck fully opened flowers from the male parent block in the early morning.

steps.description: collected in polythene bag in early morning hours of the crossing day.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plucked male flowers in a polythene bag.

steps.description: The pollen separated from anthers was filtered through muslin cloth into another cup.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate pollen from the anthers of collected flowers.

steps.description: The pollen separated from anthers was filtered through muslin cloth into another cup.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter the separated pollen through muslin cloth into a cup.

steps.description: The extracted pollens were then transferred to plastic vials by using camel hair brush.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer the filtered pollen into plastic vials using a camel hair brush.

steps.description: The plastic vials were kept in a cool place for overnight period under refrigerated condition.
steps.searchable_summary: Store the plastic vials containing pollen in a cool place under refrigeration overnight (for stored pollen treatments).

methods.description: Process for applying collected pollen (fresh or stored) to the stigma of emasculated female flowers at specific time intervals.

steps.description: The fresh pollens extracted from male parental flowers were filled up in the plastic rings specially designed for easy pollination.

steps.description: The stigma of previously emasculated female buds was gently dipped in pollen mass of plastic ring borne on left hand forefinger.
steps.searchable_summary: Gently dip the stigma of previously emasculated female buds into the pollen mass in the plastic ring.
